concepts.cultural_hint: A bright pink elephant calmly sipping tea from a tiny clay kulhad at a roadside Assamese tea stall, whimsical and instantly recognizable, with no text or logos.
A village goat perched proudly on an old bicycle leaning against a mud wall in a Northeast Indian courtyard, funny but clear at phone size.
A calm cow draped in a red-bordered Assamese gamosa like a scarf, standing in a village courtyard without any writing.
A duck wearing an oversized Assamese jaapi hat beside a bamboo-edged village pond, absurd and unmistakable.
A crow mid-snatch lifting orange jalebi from a steel plate at an outdoor sweet stall in an Indian market lane.
A small frog sitting like a drummer on an upright Assamese Bihu dhol against a bamboo wall.
A water buffalo sitting politely in a wooden river boat as if waiting for a ferry on a muddy Northeast riverside.
An Indian peacock perched atop a wooden handcart piled with vegetables in a lively but text-free market lane.
A monkey carefully balancing a brass kalash water pot on its head near a stone-rimmed village well.
A tortoise wearing oversized rubber slippers on a dusty village path after monsoon rain.
A goat politely eating from a banana-leaf Assamese rice meal laid out on a woven mat in a domestic courtyard.
A bright pink elephant carefully crossing a narrow bamboo footbridge over a green Northeast stream, whimsical and phone-readable.
